patches.hypothesis: the patch introduced overfitting and failed to address the root cause of the method's incorrect functionality. It also introduced hardcoded values and edge case issues. The patch was refined to correct the correct the root cause of the issue, address edge case handling, and remove hardcoded values.
patches.changes: - the code was refactored to remvove edge case issues, changes variable names to reflect intended broader functionality, and removed assignment when the deduped code meets tbe condition i.e is a simple name, string, or number. + improved error handling, made method return, improved error messaging, added more test inspection, made tests insensible to external changes
patches.diff: - the code was refactored to remvove edge case issues, changes variable names to reflect intended broader functionality, and removed assignment when the deduped code meets tbe condition i.e is a simple name, string, or number. + improved error handling, made method return, improved error messaging, added more test inspection, made tests insensible to external changes
patches.original_hypothesis: The bug in the method is caused by a missing null check in the recursive call, which causes a NullPointerException when the method is called with a null argument.
